importantNotes: ⚠️ FOREIGN PILOTS: To operate a drone weighing more than 500 grams in Turkey, you must apply to the General Directorate of Civil Aviation (SHGM) through diplomatic channels at least 20 business days before the flight, obtain insurance, and fly under the supervision of a Turkish citizen holding an İHA-2 license. Drones weighing less than 500 g (such as the DJI Mini, Flip, etc.) are exempt from these requirements.
💰 PENALTY WARNING: Flying without a registration will result in a fine of approximately 78,701 TL. Fines increase annually in line with the revaluation rate; equipment may be confiscated.
🗺️ ZONE SYSTEM: Turkey is divided into green (open), yellow (permit required), and red (prohibited) zones. Be sure to check the zone status at iha.shgm.gov.tr before your flight.

🏛️ HISTORIC AND TOURISTIC SITES: Flying is prohibited over the Historic Peninsula of Istanbul (Sultanahmet, Hagia Sophia, Topkapı, Grand Bazaar), certain valleys in Cappadocia, Pamukkale, Ephesus, and other archaeological sites without special permission from the Ministry of Culture and Tourism.

🛂 IMPORTS AND CUSTOMS: If you are entering Turkey with drones weighing more than 500 g, you may be required to declare them at customs. Imports for commercial purposes require a separate process; the Bosphorus, military ports, and border regions are subject to additional restrictions.

importantNotes: 📋 DUAL REGULATORY FRAMEWORK — DAN 91 vs. DAN 151: Chile distinguishes between flying in UNPOPULATED areas (DAN 91, more permissive; recreational flights ≤750 g without a permit) and flying in POPULATED areas or areas of public interest (DAN 151, which requires registration, a certified pilot, and insurance). The applicable regulations depend on the location AND purpose.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: The rules apply equally to non-residents. Registration is not required for recreational drone flights with drones weighing ≤750 g in unpopulated private areas. For any other operations (tourism, professional use, near people, in parks), the same permits are required as for Chilean citizens.

🌋 NATIONAL PARKS AND RESERVES: CONAF (National Forestry Corporation) prohibits drones without a special permit in parks such as Torres del Paine, Lauca, and Isluga Volcano National Park. Chilean Antarctica: special regulations from INACH. Check with each park or reserve before traveling.

🏔️ RESTRICTED AREAS: Downtown Santiago (La Moneda, Plaza de Armas), SCEL Airport (Pudahuel), military and border zones. Easter Island, Robinson Crusoe Island, Antarctica: additional restrictions. See AIP Chile (ENR 5.1) for a map of prohibited, restricted, and hazardous areas.

💰 ANNUAL RENEWAL: DGAC registration is valid for 12 months and must be renewed annually. Operating without a valid registration or credential may result in a fine, confiscation of equipment, and a ban on operations.

importantNotes: 📋 RAC 100 (currently in effect): the main regulation governing drones in Colombia. It replaces the previous RAC 91, Appendix 13. It establishes the Open Category (operation without prior authorization) and the Specific Category (with authorization). Circular 100-MAUT-5.0-22-010-CI provides step-by-step guidance on the registration process.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: The regulations also apply to non-residents. Drones with an MTOW of 200 g or more must be registered before operating in Colombian territory. A passport and photos of the equipment (6 images in a PDF file under 2 MB) are the basic requirements. Drones weighing less than 200 g (DJI Mini, Flip) are exempt from registration, but all other rules apply.

🌳 NATIONAL PARKS AND PROTECTED AREAS: Colombia’s National Natural Parks (PNN) prohibit drones without special permission in parks such as Tayrona, Cocuy, Los Nevados, and Amacayacu. The Colombian Caribbean and the Amazon region have additional restrictions—check with each park before your trip.

🏛️ RESTRICTED AND URBAN AREAS: Downtown Bogotá (Casa de Nariño, Plaza Bolívar, El Dorado Airport (SKBO)), Cartagena’s walled city, military zones, borders (Venezuela, Ecuador, Panama), navigable rivers, and oil rigs are subject to permanent restrictions—check Aerocivil’s GEO zones.

💰 PENALTIES AND LIABILITY: Operating without a license or outside permitted areas may result in significant fines (UVT — Tax Value Unit), confiscation of equipment, and, in serious cases, criminal liability. For commercial use: liability insurance is required.

keyRules: Maximum altitude: 120 m (400 ft) above ground level (AGL)
Minimum distance of 8 km from airports and airfields
A minimum distance of 30 m from people, animals, and buildings not involved
ALL drones weighing less than 25 kg must have an identification plate with the serial number, owner's name, and phone number

importantNotes: 🏷️ MANDATORY IDENTIFICATION PLATE: Although drones weighing less than 25 kg for recreational use do NOT require formal registration, ALL drones must have an identification plate with a serial number, the owner’s name, and a contact phone number. This is the strictest requirement for physical identification.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: The rules apply to non-residents. For recreational use: identification tag + follow basic rules (no airports, no private property, no prisons) — no formal registration required. Customs may request a drone declaration upon entry into the country.

🌳 NATIONAL PARKS AND SINAC: The National System of Conservation Areas (SINAC) requires a special permit for drones in parks such as Manuel Antonio, Corcovado, Tortuguero, Arenal Volcano, Monteverde, and Poás Volcano. Biodiversity and wildlife are protected—check with each park BEFORE your trip.

🏖️ TOURIST AREAS AND AIRPORTS: SJO (Juan Santamaría, Alajuela), LIR (Liberia, Guanacaste), the Presidential Palace, embassies, and military zones have permanent restrictions. Tourist beaches such as Tamarindo, Manuel Antonio, and Jacó often have additional municipal restrictions.

💰 COMMERCIAL USE AND PENALTIES: Commercial flights without an RPAS license or a certificate from the Technical Council may result in fines, confiscation of equipment, and a ban on operations. For paid photography/video work: Obtain the license before beginning the activity.

The registration number or QR code must be visible on the drone

importantNotes: 📋 RDAC 101 (effective as of December 2024): new UAS regulation approved by the DGAC Standards Committee on October 24, 2024. Applies to ALL individuals (recreational, private, professional) operating drones with a maximum takeoff weight (MTOW) of up to 250 kg. ELIMINATES the previous weight-based exemption under Regulation 2020-0110.

🆔 REGISTRATION IS MANDATORY FOR EVERYONE: Unlike most Latin American countries, Ecuador has NO weight threshold—even a DJI Mini (<250 g) or a toy drone must be registered with SISAC. The system issues a certificate with a unique QR code.

🐢 GALÁPAGOS — STRICT RESTRICTIONS: Recreational drones are PROHIBITED throughout the archipelago. You can bring your drone (it will not be confiscated at customs), but you CANNOT fly it. Operations are permitted only with special authorization for: scientific research, rescue, environmental monitoring, and commercial/educational audiovisual production. Fines and deportation are possible.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: The rules apply equally to non-residents. Before traveling: Register your drone with SISAC (you may be asked to provide an Ecuadorian ID or passport, along with the drone’s specifications). If you’re visiting the Galápagos, consider leaving your drone on the mainland—you won’t be able to fly it there.

🌳 OTHER NATIONAL PARKS: Cotopaxi, Yasuní, Cajas, Sangay, and other protected areas managed by the Ministry of the Environment require specific permits for drones. Check with the Ministry of the Environment and the relevant park before flying.

keyRules: Maximum height depends on weight: ≤250 g → 30 m; 250 g–25 kg → 60 m; 25–150 kg → 120 m
Drones weighing more than 250 g (all uses) require AAC registration — cost: US$5; in-person registration at Albrook

importantNotes: 📏 ALTITUDES VARY BY WEIGHT (unique in Latin America): Panama’s RACP has three maximum altitude levels based on drone weight: drones ≤250 g are limited to ONLY 30 m, drones weighing 250 g–25 kg are limited to 60 m, and only drones weighing ≥25 kg can reach the standard 120 m. Check your drone’s weight before planning your flight altitude.

✈️ TOURISTS — 14-DAY RULE: If your stay in Panama is ≤14 calendar days, you can apply for special AAC authorization without permanently registering the drone. Stays >14 days: AAC registration is mandatory for drones >250 g. Recreational drones ≤250 g are exempt in both cases.

🚢 PANAMA CANAL — SPECIAL RESTRICTION: The Canal basin (including the locks, Gatún Lake, and adjacent areas) requires a mandatory Letter of Informed Consent from the Canal Authority prior to any flight. The AAC will NOT authorize operations over this area without this letter. This also applies to private property, indigenous reserves, and protected areas.

🌳 PROTECTED AREAS AND MIAMBIENTE: National parks such as Soberanía, Volcán Barú, Coiba (a UNESCO World Heritage Site), and Darién require a specific permit from the Ministry of the Environment (MiAmbiente). The islands of Bocas del Toro and Guna Yala also have restrictions—coordinate with the local community before operating.

🏢 IN-PERSON REGISTRATION REQUIRED: Unlike most countries, AAC registration must be done IN PERSON at the Albrook office (Building 805). Bring: identification, the physical drone, the completed form, and US$5. Check appointment availability and processing times before your trip.

keyRules: Drones weighing ≥250 g (0.55 lb): mandatory registration with IDAC via Vuela Seguro (RAD-48)

importantNotes: 📋 LEGAL FRAMEWORK: Drone operations are regulated by Civil Aviation Law 491-06 + RAD-107 (small unmanned aircraft system) + RAD-48 (registration and labeling). The IDAC (Dominican Civil Aviation Institute) is the competent authority.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: These rules apply to non-residents. Drones weighing ≥250 g must be registered before operation. For recreational use of drones weighing ≤250 g (DJI Mini, Flip): registration is not required, but all other rules apply. Documentation is recommended upon entry into the country.

🏖️ PUNTA CANA AND COASTAL TOURIST AREAS: The airports PUJ (Punta Cana), SDQ (Las Américas), and STI (Santiago) have a 9-km exclusion zone. Saona, Catalina, Bayahibe, Las Galeras: Many beaches and resorts have additional restrictions—check with your hotel or tour operator before flying.

🌳 PROTECTED AREAS: Los Haitises, del Este (Saona), Sierra de Bahoruco, Jaragua, and Lago Enriquillo National Parks require a special permit from the Ministry of the Environment. Samaná Bay (whale watching, Jan–Mar): Additional restrictions are in place to avoid disturbing wildlife.

🏛️ PERMANENTLY RESTRICTED AREAS: National Palace (Santo Domingo), Ministry of Defense, all military bases (San Isidro, Las Calderas), and the Dominican Oil Refinery. The Colonial Zone (UNESCO World Heritage Site in Santo Domingo) has temporary restrictions—check beforehand.

Register as an operator if the drone weighs ≥250 g or is equipped with a camera or sensor. Private individuals must be at least 18 years old. The fee consists of a registration fee plus an annual maintenance fee—check the current amounts with the Swedish Transport Agency.

An A1/A3 drone license is required if the drone weighs 250 g or more — online theory test administered by the Swedish Transport Agency (for a fee; the license is valid for 5 years). Minimum age for operating a drone independently: 15 years. Those under 15 may fly only with a valid drone license under the supervision of a remote pilot aged 16 or older with a valid drone license.
The operator number must be clearly marked on the drone.
Keep the drone within visual line of sight (VLOS)—do not fly around corners or behind buildings.
A1: Drones weighing less than 250 g — fly over uninvolved individuals (not crowds).
A2: Drones weighing up to 4 kg — at least 30 m from bystanders (5 m in low-speed mode). Requires an A2 certificate.
A3: Drones weighing up to 25 kg — at least 150 m from residential, commercial, industrial, and recreational areas.
Never fly over crowds without special permission.
Check the LFV Drone Map and NOTAMs before each flight.
importantNotes: As of January 1, 2026, it is a criminal offense to fly a drone weighing 250 g or more without a valid drone license—punishable by a fine or up to six months in prison.
Nature reserves and national parks often have their own restrictions—check with the County Administrative Board for the specific area.
Protected sites (military bases, prisons, power plants, etc.) — flying is prohibited without a permit. Marked on the Drone Map.
In the event of an accident or incident involving a drone weighing more than 250 g — report it to the Swedish Transport Agency via an incident report.
Insurance is strongly recommended—some home insurance policies cover drone flights, while others do not.

keyRules: In Ukraine, during martial law, the civilian use of drones is prohibited—flights without military authorization are illegal.
An unidentified drone may be perceived as a threat. There is a real danger to life and safety—do not fly.
GPS and GNSS systems are experiencing disruptions nationwide. Never rely on satellite positioning.
Are you in the EU? This app shows the official airspace zones of 19 European countries—in Ukrainian.
We will update the app to provide full support on the same day that civilian flights are permitted again.
importantNotes: While martial law is in effect, information about Ukraine is limited to security-related data.
The State Aviation Service (Derzhaviavsluzhba) is the official source of information on aviation regulations in Ukraine.
Ukrainian citizens can register as drone operators in an EASA member state and legally operate drones throughout the EU.
